prerequisites: A CircleCI account with access to the organization and project you want to sync.
A CircleCI personal API token, created from User settings > Personal API Tokens (provided in the api_key field).
The CircleCI organization identifier for org_id and the project identifier for project_id.
A start_date marking the earliest point from which to sync data.
Optionally, a specific workflow_id and/or job_number if you want to scope the sync to a particular workflow or job.

authMethods.name: Personal API token (api_key)
authMethods.summary: Authentication uses a CircleCI personal API token supplied in the api_key field. Create it in the CircleCI app under User settings > Personal API Tokens > Create New Token; it grants full read and write permissions on the CircleCI v1 and v2 APIs. The connector sends this token to authenticate API v2 requests, which CircleCI accepts via the Circle-Token header or an Authorization: Bearer header.

setupSteps.title: Create a personal API token
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the CircleCI application, open User settings, select Personal API Tokens, then click Create New Token. Copy the generated token immediately, as it is shown only once. This token has full read and write permissions on the CircleCI APIs.

setupSteps.title: Identify your organization and project
setupSteps.body: Determine the org_id (your CircleCI organization identifier) and project_id (the project identifier) for the project you want to sync. In CircleCI API v2 these correspond to the identifiers used to build a project slug; for GitHub App, GitLab, and Bitbucket Data Center these are opaque string IDs.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the personal API token as api_key, then provide the required org_id, project_id, and start_date. Optionally set workflow_id and job_number to scope the sync to a specific workflow or job. start_date is required and bounds how far back data is retrieved.

setupSteps.title: Verify connectivity
setupSteps.body: Save the configuration and run the connection check. A successful check confirms the api_key authenticates against the CircleCI API v2 and that the org_id and project_id resolve to an accessible project.
